authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Plaskett Meadows Pond not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Plaskett Meadows Pond not listed)
2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (Updated 06/01/2026)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon — CDFW
sources.quotedText: R2 Glenn Plaskett Meadows Pond Rainbow Trout Catchable Put and Take June-August; R2 Glenn Plaskett Meadows Pond Rainbow Trout Sub-catchable Put and Grow June-August

sources.title: Mendocino National Forest | Plaskett Meadows Campground | Forest Service
sources.quotedText: There are two small lakes for trout fishing, three to four acres each. No motorized boats allowed. Swimming is not recommended.

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Valleys/Flats layer record for gaz_id 264839, "Plaskett Meadows", Flat, Glenn County, CA (nearest authoritative named GNIS feature; the pond itself has no separate GNIS lake/reservoir entry)
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":264839,"gaz_name":"Plaskett Meadows","gaz_featureclass":"Flat","fcode":15028,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Glenn"}; geometry (Web Mercator -13674797.0112, 4826324.6072) converts to lon -122.842792, lat 39.726827, roughly 100 m from the file's recorded coordinate (-122.8439, 39.7265) — the pond has no separate GNIS lake entry (queried MapServer/7 for "Plaskett Meadows Pond" and "Plaskett Meadow Lake" with no results); this is the nearest authoritative named GNIS feature confirming the location.

parameters.note: Plaskett Meadows Pond is not listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide regulation for lakes, reservoirs, and ponds in §5.85(a)(1).

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year (general statewide lake/pond regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds: five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.description: Plaskett Meadows Pond is one of two small trout-fishing lakes beside the USDA Forest Service Plaskett Meadows Campground in the Mendocino National Forest; motorized boats are not allowed and swimming is not recommended.

regulations.citation: Mendocino National Forest — Plaskett Meadows Campground
regulations.humanSummary: No motorized boats allowed; swimming not recommended (USFS)
regulations.verbatimText: No motorized boats allowed. Swimming is not recommended.

stockingSchedule.note: CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon lists Plaskett Meadows Pond (Region 2, Glenn Co.) for catchable-size Rainbow Trout under the Put and Take program, with a stated release month range of June-August. This is CDFW's provisional plan for 2026, not a guaranteed annual constant.

CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon lists Plaskett Meadows Pond (Region 2, Glenn Co.) for sub-catchable-size Rainbow Trout under the Put and Grow program, with a stated release month range of June-August. This is CDFW's provisional plan for 2026, not a guaranteed annual constant.
